name: Sharanga
description: Sharanga is a divine bow of immense power, primarily associated with the Hindu god Vishnu, in his avatar as Rama. Forged by the celestial architect Vishwakarma, Sharanga is not merely a weapon but an embodiment of divine justice and cosmic order. Its construction involved the use of celestial materials and divine energies, rendering it virtually indestructible and capable of unleashing devastating power. The bow is described as being adorned with precious gems and emitting a radiant light, symbolizing its divine nature. The sound of its string being drawn is said to resemble the roar of thunder, instilling fear in the hearts of even the most formidable adversaries. Sharanga is more than just a bow; it represents Rama's righteousness and his commitment to upholding dharma (righteous conduct). It is a symbol of his ability to vanquish evil and restore balance to the world. The arrows released from Sharanga were infallible, possessing the power to seek out their targets with unerring accuracy and deliver devastating blows.
subtitle: The Divine Bow of Vishnu/Rama
origin: Forged by Vishwakarma using celestial materials and divine energy.
currentLocation: Lost; possibly returned to the celestial realm after Rama's departure.

keyMyths.title: Rama's Victory Over Ravana
keyMyths.description: Sharanga played a crucial role in the epic battle between Rama and Ravana. Rama, wielding Sharanga, unleashed a barrage of divine arrows that pierced Ravana's armor and ultimately led to his demise. The bow's unfailing accuracy and devastating power were instrumental in defeating the ten-headed demon king, restoring peace and order to the world. The final arrow, the Brahmastra, was launched from Sharanga.

keyMyths.title: The String's Unbreakable Nature
keyMyths.description: Legends speak of the Sharanga's string as being unbreakable, a testament to its divine construction. Attempts to damage or sever the string were futile, further solidifying its reputation as an unparalleled weapon. This unbreakable nature symbolizes the unwavering commitment to dharma that Rama embodies.

keyMyths.title: The Sound of the Bow
keyMyths.description: The sound produced when drawing the string of Sharanga was said to be akin to the roar of thunder. This sound alone was enough to instill fear and dread in the hearts of enemies, signifying the immense power and divine presence associated with the bow and its wielder. It served as a warning of impending doom for those who opposed Rama.

sources.author: Valmiki
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Rama and the Sharanga bow. Provides detailed accounts of its use in battle.
sources.text: The primary source for the story of Rama and the Sharanga bow. Provides detailed accounts of its use in battle.

sources.author: Tulsidas
sources.description: A later retelling of the Ramayana, offering further insights into the significance and power of Sharanga.

sources.author: Swami Sivananda
sources.work: Lord Rama: Life, Teachings and Philosophy
sources.description: A modern commentary on the life and significance of Rama, including the role of Sharanga in his divine mission.
